title: Deduplicate Near-Identical Strings
description: Remove near-duplicate chunks from retrieved RAG results based on simple normalized text comparison.
problem: Write dedupe_chunks(chunks) that removes duplicates after lowercasing and stripping whitespace/punctuation, keeping the first occurrence's original text and order.
starter_code:
import re

def dedupe_chunks(chunks):
    # Normalize each chunk (lowercase, strip non-alphanumeric) to detect duplicates
    # Keep first occurrence (original text) for each unique normalized form
    return chunks
hint: Normalize with re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]', '', chunk.lower()). Track seen normalized forms in a set; only keep chunks whose normalized form hasn't been seen.
